newsletters.title: Why SaaS freemium playbooks don't work in AI, and what to do instead
newsletters.wiki_relevance: AI monetization strategy article. Discusses why traditional SaaS freemium fails when compute costs scale with usage. Proposes 3-pillar model: gate usage intensity, gate outcomes (agents), gate heavy compute modalities. More business/strategy than technical — limited wiki value for a technical LLM/Agent knowledge base.
recommended_actions: Optional: reference in AI business models concept page
Note: Vikas Kansal (Google AI Product Lead) could be an entity to track

newsletters.title: Doing Vibe Physics — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI
newsletters.wiki_relevance: Latent.Space podcast interview with Alex Lupsasca (OpenAI researcher, 2024 Breakthrough Prize winner). GPT-5.x reproducing physics papers in 30 minutes that took humans months/years. 'Vibe Physics' methodology — AI extending human knowledge frontier in theoretical physics. Case studies: gluon tree amplitudes (32-term equation solved in 1 week), graviton paper (110 pages of novel physics in 3 days). Includes arXiv paper references and full AI session transcript.
recommended_actions: Create entity page for alex-lupsasca (OpenAI researcher, Breakthrough Prize winner)
Create concept page for 'vibe-physics' / AI for Science methodology
Document GPT-5.x science capabilities benchmark
Reference arXiv papers: 2602.12176 (gluons), 2603.04330 (gravitons)
Update OpenAI research entity page with science breakthrough examples
Create concept page for 'AI for theoretical physics'
